author: opus perf engineer (worker/opus/v0120-perf)

case.init_mode: standalone_native_init (IC from wrfinput_d01, LBC from wrfbdy_d01; NO CPU-WRF wrfout dependency)
case.precision: fp64 (force_fp64=True; the operational standalone path forces pure fp64 for the acoustic solve per daily_pipeline.py:265 / ADR F7)

cache_hit_clean_measurement.implied_compile_via_cache_s: ~10 (29.26 hour-1 - 16.71 first-execute - ~2.4 warmup); a CACHE READ, vs a cold XLA compile of >100s (the full-pipeline cold hour-1 was 147.6s, ~130s of which is compile). => the persistent cache turns a >100s cold compile into a ~10s disk-read on every subsequent run, ZERO numerics change (bit-identical executable).
cache_hit_clean_measurement.method: proofs/perf/v0120_profile_driver.py on an isolated cache_iso2 ALREADY POPULATED by a prior cold run (same program), fresh process = guaranteed cache HIT, NO restart/repeatability pipeline probes.

cold_compile_vs_cache.method: Same case/process, --hours metric isolates hour-1 (compile-bearing) from warm hours. Cold = GPUWRF_JAX_CACHE=0 (cache fully disabled). Cache-populate = empty isolated JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR. Cache-hit = fresh process on the now-warm isolated cache, measured cleanly via the profiling driver (29.26s, see cache_hit_clean_measurement).
cold_compile_vs_cache.note: The full-pipeline hour-1 (147.6s cold / 140.8s warm-default) conflates compile + ~16.7s first-execute + JAX warmup + the pipeline's restart/repeatability probes, so it is a NOISY upper-bound. The CLEAN driver measurement above (no probes, guaranteed cache hit) is the load-bearing number: cache-hit hour-1 = 29.26s vs a cold compile >100s.

fp32_fast_path.finding: The precision matrix (src/gpuwrf/contracts/precision.py) stores u/v/theta/qv/hydrometeors as FP32_GATED and mu/p/ph/w/qke/surface-stability as FP64, BUT the operational standalone path sets force_fp64=True (daily_pipeline.py:265), which upcasts every prognostic to fp64 for the acoustic solve (fp32 detonates the perturbation per the F7 gates). So the CLI standalone forecast runs PURE fp64. A gated-fp32 operational path is a future ADR-007 decision and is documented as 'currently no faster on this memory-bound workload' (docs/resource-profile.md). No fp32 standalone number is reported because none is reachable via the CLI.
fp32_fast_path.status: NOT on the standalone CLI path

gpu_measurements_resolved.coupled_graph_flag_ab: MEASURED - the flag is a 0.84x REGRESSION on the coupled path (see safe_speedup_graph_capture below); NOT landed.
gpu_measurements_resolved.hlo_op_histogram: Not captured via the driver (the segmented host-loop entry is not jit-lowerable); the authoritative warmed-step op/kernel breakdown is the existing nsys CSVs, ranked in v0120_profile.md.
gpu_measurements_resolved.note: All initially-deferred GPU-gated items were CAPTURED once the concurrent agents' 24h jobs cleared the GPU lock (one-GPU discipline honored throughout - no contended measurement). Results:

peak_vram_note: 4727 MB (4.6 GiB) measured via memory_stats peak_bytes_in_use on the d01 9km standalone fp64 path (peak after 2 forecast hours). Far below the documented d02 3km ~24.6 GiB (docs/resource-profile.md) - d01 is a smaller/coarser grid. A 32 GiB RTX 5090 has ample headroom for d01.

reconciliation_with_denominator.do_not_inflate: This d01 measurement CONFIRMS the warm-throughput family; it does NOT establish a new headline. Cite speedup_denominator.md for the speedup factor.
reconciliation_with_denominator.interpretation: d01 (9km, 93x59) warm = 16.69 s/fc-hr is IN-FAMILY with the established d02 (3km, 159x66) numerator 15.35-16.39 s/fc-hr. Same dt=10s / 10-substep / 180-step-radiation structure; per-step cost is dominated by the launch-bound step structure, not grid size, at these GPU-underutilized sizes. No NEW speedup ratio is claimed for d01: there is no clean uncontended CPU-WRF standalone d01 denominator. The honest published speedup remains the d02 number: ~5x apples-to-apples (band 5-8x), dt-parity floor ~3.2x, real-user warm ~2.5x.

reproduce.note_per_hour: wall_clock_per_hour_s[0] = XLA compile (cold or cache-read) + first-hour execute + JAX warmup; wall_clock_per_hour_s[1:] = WARM steady-state. Case-build is separate (wall_clock_total_s - sum(per_hour)).
reproduce.warm_and_cold: /tmp/wrf_gpu_run.sh taskset -c 0-3 env PYTHONPATH=src JAX_ENABLE_X64=true XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=false [GPUWRF_JAX_CACHE_DIR=<dir> | GPUWRF_JAX_CACHE=0] python -m gpuwrf.cli run --input-dir <case> --output-dir <scratch> --domain d01 --hours 3

coupled_full_physics_standalone_ab_MEASU.also_costs: cold compile rose 29s (baseline) -> 137s (gms1) because the flag changes the cache key (cold compile) AND graph-capturing more segments lengthens compile.
coupled_full_physics_standalone_ab_MEASU.interpretation: On the FULL COUPLED standalone d01 path (physics ON), --xla_gpu_graph_min_graph_size=1 is ~19% SLOWER (0.84x), NOT faster. The prior sprint's 1.71x was dynamics-ONLY (run_physics=False); that win does NOT carry to the coupled step - the physics couplers' graph structure does not benefit from lowering the capture threshold, and the extra graph-capture/overhead hurts. This is exactly the coupled-leg confirmation the prior sprint deferred, and it FALSIFIES the carry-over assumption.
coupled_full_physics_standalone_ab_MEASU.numerics: BIT-IDENTICAL final state (u/v/theta/w/ph_total/mu_total/qv all dmax=dmean=0.0, rel_L2=0), all_finite both. So the flag is safe numerically - it is simply not a speedup on the coupled path.
coupled_full_physics_standalone_ab_MEASU.provenance: proofs/perf/v0120_flag_baseline.json (warm 16.71, finite) vs v0120_flag_gms1.json (warm 19.81, finite, XLA_FLAGS=--xla_gpu_graph_min_graph_size=1). Raw scratch copies under <DATA_ROOT>/wrf_perf_scratch/.
coupled_full_physics_standalone_ab_MEASU.recommendation: Do NOT set --xla_gpu_graph_min_graph_size=1 in the operational launch env. Keep XLA defaults. The launch-tax reduction must instead target the COUPLED graph (future work: per-coupler fusion / a coupled-aware command-buffer config), or be re-measured if a dynamics-only fast path is ever shipped standalone.
coupled_full_physics_standalone_ab_MEASU.verdict: DO NOT LAND - the flag is a REGRESSION on the coupled full-physics standalone path.

safe_speedup_graph_capture.rationale: Lowers the CUDA-graph capture threshold from XLA default 5 to 1, capturing the dycore's many short (<5-op) dependent fusion chains into CUDA graphs and batching the thousands of tiny launches (cuLaunchKernelEx = 38% of CUDA API time; cuGraphLaunch only 5.5% today). Prior dynamics-ONLY probe: 1.71x (fusion_results.md).

summary: Honest, reproducible benchmark of the v0.12.0 standalone CLI path (gpuwrf.cli run). All numbers measured on one RTX 5090, fp64 (force_fp64=True on the operational standalone path), dt=10s. Profiling, not validation: short runs only.

warm_throughput.provenance: 6 independent warm hours across 3 separate processes, all 16.68-16.72 s. Reproducible to <0.1 s.
